item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The MTF kid's show idea was really cute

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Until Addendum-3, I thought this was a video site from a "broken masquerade" universe; I'm disappointed that it wasn't.

item.title: Re: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Author here; I'm happy with the content of these changes, though it might make more sense to change the word "aired" to "featured" or something. It's a streaming service, not broadcast television, after all.

item.title: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
edit: edit made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show Proposed edits
- Hide

To link to another skip featuring an SCP themed television show, I propose the following edit:

Notable for the fact that, with a fair degree of accuracy, it depicts actual containment events as they occur in Foundation records, albeit in a highly exaggerated fashion.

to

Notable for the fact that, with a fair degree of accuracy, it depicts actual containment events as they occur in Foundation records, albeit in a highly exaggerated fashion.1

Footnotes
1. On 12/14/16 this program featured a special purporting to be a crossover with Agent Danny of the SCP, the current manifestation of SCP-1257. As a result, further links between the two are under investigation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have the same feelsings Petrograd has. This is just a combination of a lot of things other skips have done and quite frankly, I'm more interested in stuff like the Police Academy sequels than stalking some guy and killing him through stream chat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mmmmmmm… This has been done before, pretty frequently. One of the first things people seem to go to after "TV from elsewhere!" is "Knows about the Foundation zomg!" (E.g., SCP-2702,SCP-2238 both mirror major portions of this SCP). It's kind of a cheap way to piggyback on SCP lore and show that this is threatening to the Foundation without doing any work to establish itself. (I mean, the police academy thing was amusing, but that's just one thing.)

"Knows about containment and is pissed" is an even older trope.

I'd feel better about this if it was telling some new or interesting story, but that's pretty much it. The stuff about the commentators potentially harming and killing the site director (although we also have an evil prank show) was entertaining enough to bump me up to a no-vote, but that's as far as I go.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel a wonderful broken masquerade story could be written with this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One minor nitpick - names of works (movies, TV shows, etc.) should be italicized. Other than that, no problems.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Car Downloaders is great, but otherwise it's a thing what spookily sees into Foundation life. Seems to be pretty unilaterally focused on them, too, or is that just what's important for reporting in the documentation, in-universe? Not quite catching my fancy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had considered adding more videos to the log, but I didn't want them to become the focus of the article. If people think it would benefit from more, I'll happily add them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, regular twitch chat but they kill with anomalies instead of SWAT teams?

Joking aside, there were a few things that put me off enough for a neutral vote. I think you could have expanded the list of videos a little, because that's what usually catches my interest with articles like these, and the addenda were a little heavy with redactions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this a hell of a lot better than I thought I would when I saw the number of Zs and Xs in the title.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So this is my first post to the main list. I've drafted a few ideas before but this is the first one I liked enough to put up here.
Thanks to everyone who gave feedback in #site19, which helped me nudge this into the direction it ended up going.

item.title: Take My upvote
item.content:encoded:
I smiled when i saw MTF Mu-7 ("Car Downloaders"). I upvoted when i saw Mobile Task Force Extreme.
+1
